title: taraxacum officinale subsp. laceratum
intro: Taraxacum officinale subsp. laceratum, also known as the serrated dandelion or Japanese dandelion, is a subspecies of the common dandelion plant, characterized by its serrated leaves and yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve liver and kidney issues. The health benefits of Taraxacum officinale subsp. laceratum include reducing blood pressure, improving liver function, and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Taraxacum officinale subsp. laceratum can be prepared as herbal tea, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional cooking as a leafy green vegetable.
